title: medicago murex subsp. heterocarpa
intro: Medicago murex subsp. heterocarpa, also known as the hairy medick or hairy lucerne, is a perennial legume plant native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which can aid in the prevention and treatment of infections. The health benefits of Medicago murex subsp. heterocarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in traditional medicine, typically in the form of infusions or decoctions, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
